reasoning: FRED data indicates 2026-01-02 effective yield closed at 4.85%, showing strong upward momentum (up 7 bps over 3 days). A "yield shock" on Jan 2 pushed the 10-year Treasury up ~20 bps due to fiscal expansion fears and a hawkish Fed Dot Plot (median 1 rate cut in 2026 vs market expectations of 3). This creates significant upward pressure on corporate yields in the short term (Jan 11), leading to an initial forecast >0.5. For longer-term dates (Feb 3, Apr 4), uncertainty rises regarding whether tighter spreads might offset Treasury moves, or if weaker Q2 economic data will pull rates down, so estimates taper smoothly towards 0.50 in alignment with the directive to regress probabilities over time.
